_note: Curated MMA history. Keys are MM-DD (month-day). Easy to edit/extend. Dates are high-confidence iconic events.

01-18.text: **UFC 246** — Conor McGregor returned and TKO'd Donald Cerrone in just 40 seconds.

02-02.text: **UFC 81: Breaking Point** — Brock Lesnar made his UFC debut (losing to Frank Mir), and Antonio Rodrigo Nogueira beat Tim Sylvia for the interim heavyweight title.

02-23.text: **UFC 157** — Ronda Rousey vs Liz Carmouche became the first-ever women's fight in the UFC; Rousey won by armbar.

03-05.text: **UFC 196** — Nate Diaz shocked Conor McGregor by submission, and Miesha Tate submitted Holly Holm for the bantamweight title.

03-19.text: **UFC 128** — Jon Jones beat Mauricio 'Shogun' Rua to become, at 23, the youngest champion in UFC history.

04-09.text: **The Ultimate Fighter 1 Finale** — Forrest Griffin vs Stephan Bonnar, the slugfest widely credited with saving the UFC and launching it on cable TV.

07-03.text: **UFC 116** — Brock Lesnar survived a brutal first round to submit Shane Carwin and unify the heavyweight title.

07-06.text: **UFC 162** — Chris Weidman knocked out Anderson Silva, ending Silva's record 16-fight UFC win streak and 2,457-day title reign.

07-07.text: **UFC 148** — Anderson Silva beat Chael Sonnen in their grudge-match rematch.
**UFC 226** — Daniel Cormier KO'd Stipe Miocic to become a simultaneous two-division champion.

07-09.text: **UFC 200** — Amanda Nunes submitted Miesha Tate for the title; Brock Lesnar beat Mark Hunt; Daniel Cormier beat Anderson Silva.

07-10.text: **UFC 264** — Dustin Poirier beat Conor McGregor after McGregor suffered a broken leg in their trilogy bout.

07-11.text: **UFC 100** — Brock Lesnar beat Frank Mir, Georges St-Pierre dominated Thiago Alves, and Dan Henderson flattened Michael Bisping.
**UFC 189** — Conor McGregor stopped Chad Mendes to win the interim featherweight title in front of a roaring Las Vegas crowd.

08-20.text: **UFC 202** — Conor McGregor edged Nate Diaz in their rematch by majority decision.
**UFC 278** — Leon Edwards knocked out Kamaru Usman with a 5th-round head kick to win the welterweight title.

10-06.text: **UFC 229** — Khabib Nurmagomedov submitted Conor McGregor in the biggest PPV in UFC history.

10-24.text: **UFC 254** — Khabib Nurmagomedov submitted Justin Gaethje, then retired 29-0.

11-04.text: **UFC 217** at Madison Square Garden — GSP returned to submit Michael Bisping; Rose Namajunas KO'd Joanna Jedrzejczyk; TJ Dillashaw KO'd Cody Garbrandt. Three title changes in one night.

11-12.text: **UFC 1** — the very first UFC event in Denver, Colorado. Royce Gracie won the eight-man tournament and introduced the world to Brazilian jiu-jitsu.
**UFC 205** at Madison Square Garden — Conor McGregor KO'd Eddie Alvarez to become the first fighter to hold two UFC titles at once.
**UFC 281** — Alex Pereira TKO'd Israel Adesanya in the 5th round to win the middleweight title.

11-15.text: **UFC 193** — Holly Holm head-kick KO'd Ronda Rousey in one of the biggest upsets in MMA history.

12-12.text: **UFC 194** — Conor McGregor knocked out Jose Aldo in 13 seconds, the fastest title-fight finish in UFC history.

12-28.text: **UFC 168** — Chris Weidman beat Anderson Silva again after Silva broke his leg on a checked kick; Ronda Rousey armbarred Miesha Tate.

12-29.text: **UFC 155** — Cain Velasquez dominated Junior dos Santos to reclaim the heavyweight title.
**UFC 232** — Jon Jones beat Alexander Gustafsson, and Amanda Nunes KO'd Cris Cyborg in 51 seconds.

12-30.text: **UFC 66** — Chuck Liddell TKO'd Tito Ortiz in their rematch on a landmark million-buy PPV.
**UFC 207** — Amanda Nunes KO'd Ronda Rousey in 48 seconds in Rousey's final fight.

trivia.a: 3 rounds (title fights and main events are 5)
